refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under controlled duty cycle conditions with continuous telemetry capture of cycle start/end timestamps, segmented by load, travel, and dump phases. Statistical analysis of p95 latency across minimum 100 cycles per site condition.
sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual phase time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-CT-002: The teleoperation system shall maintain operator command-to-vehicle-response latency ≤ latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-CT-003: Safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-CT-004: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/powertrain response times and operator control loop latency through hardware tuning and network optimization; monitor cycle time via onboard instrumentation.
DP-B: Decompose cycle time into phase budgets (load, travel, dump); allocate budgets to vehicle subsystems and teleoperation; enforce via real-time telemetry feedback and adaptive safety throttling.
DP-C: Implement a cycle time performance controller that dynamically adjusts vehicle speed profiles and safety constraint margins based on real-time telemetry to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target.
DP-D: Establish a baseline cycle time model from field data; use predictive telemetry to identify bottlenecks; iteratively optimize vehicle and teleoperation parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose cycle time into phase budgets (load, travel, dump); allocate budgets to vehicle subsystems and teleoperation; enforce via real-time telemetry feedback and adaptive safety throttling.

potential_sources: Vehicle phase budget (load, travel, dump) is independent of teleoperation latency budget—decoupled by explicit time allocation.
Safety constraint overhead is isolated as a separate budget line, decoupled from nominal vehicle performance.
Telemetry feedback loop is orthogonal to phase execution—monitoring does not directly couple to control.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits weak coupling: phase budgets are allocated independently, and feedback is observational rather than tightly coupled control. This supports modularity and allows independent optimization of vehicle and teleoperation subsystems.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable; risk: vague or changing targets will invalidate budget allocation.
Assumption: Vehicle subsystems can be tuned to meet allocated phase budgets; risk: hardware limitations may make budgets infeasible.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency is measurable and controllable; risk: network variability may exceed latency budget.
Assumption: Safety constraint overhead is quantifiable and can be adaptively throttled; risk: safety margins may be non-negotiable, preventing adaptive reduction.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure has sufficient fidelity and bandwidth; risk: instrumentation gaps or network congestion may prevent real-time feedback.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly define the feedback control law for adaptive safety throttling: specify how telemetry-observed cycle time deviation triggers safety margin adjustment, with hard limits to preserve safety criticality. Add a fallback mode if adaptive throttling cannot maintain p95 ≤ CT_target.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration module responsible for orchestrating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns the phase budget allocation logic, telemetry aggregation, and adaptive safety throttling decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests cycle phase timestamps (load start/end, travel start/end, dump start/end) and vehicle state from Vehicle module; outputs aggregated cycle time metrics and KPI status to Site Manager and Maintenance dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives duty cycle definition, CT_target, and site condition parameters from Site Manager; translates into phase budgets and operational constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraint status from Safety Services; outputs adaptive safety margin adjustment commands to Safety Services to balance cycle time and safety.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for telemetry uplink and tasking downlink; latency characteristics inform teleoperation latency budget.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level orchestrator and owns the cycle time KPI achievement logic. Phase budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and adaptive safety throttling are system-level concerns that naturally reside here. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry data flows to external dashboards.

Vehicle owns phase execution (load, travel, dump) and can be allocated phase budgets, but does not own the system-level KPI orchestration or adaptive safety throttling. Cycle time monitoring would be diffused across vehicle subsystems (hydraulics, powertrain, sensors).

Teleoperation owns latency budget and command-response loop, but does not own overall cycle time KPI or phase budget allocation. Cycle time feedback would be diffused across teleoperation network and control logic.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement but not cycle time KPI. Adaptive safety throttling would require tight coupling between Safety Services and cycle time logic, creating diffusion and potential conflicts between safety and performance objectives.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of the cycle time KPI because it is the system-level integration point that coordinates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. The phase budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and adaptive safety throttling are system-level orchestration concerns that cannot be cleanly localized to any single subsystem module.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it accumulates too many orchestration responsibilities; mitigation: clearly separate phase budget allocation (system-level) from phase execution (vehicle-level) and latency management (teleoperation-level).

Risk: Adaptive safety throttling may create unsafe coupling between performance and safety; mitigation: define hard safety limits that cannot be violated by adaptive logic, and require Safety Manager approval for any safety margin reduction.

Risk: Telemetry feedback loop may introduce latency that degrades real-time control; mitigation: ensure telemetry aggregation is asynchronous and does not block vehicle or teleoperation control loops.

Risk: Diffusion of cycle time monitoring across vehicle and teleoperation subsystems may create inconsistent KPI reporting; mitigation: establish a single source of truth for cycle time metrics in NGHE Solution, with vehicle and teleoperation providing raw phase timestamps.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the boundary between NGHE Solution (system-level orchestration) and Vehicle/Teleoperation (subsystem execution) by explicitly defining: (1) what phase budget allocation logic resides in NGHE Solution vs. delegated to Vehicle, (2) how telemetry is aggregated and validated, (3) the decision logic and hard limits for adaptive safety throttling, and (4) the fallback mode if KPI cannot be achieved within safety constraints.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (phase budget decomposition with adaptive safety throttling) directly addresses the refined FR requirement to achieve p95 ≤ CT_target. The DP is decoupled (phase budgets are independent), measurable (telemetry-based feedback), and feasible (budget allocation is a standard systems engineering practice). The DP does not over-constrain the design and allows flexibility in how individual subsystems meet their allocated budgets.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Cycle time monitoring is distributed across Vehicle (phase timestamps), Teleoperation (latency), and Safety Services (constraint overhead), requiring careful telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution to avoid inconsistency.
Diffusion risk: Adaptive safety throttling couples NGHE Solution to Safety Services, potentially creating circular dependencies if Safety Services also needs to optimize for cycle time.
Diffusion risk: Phase budget allocation may require coordination across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, creating implicit dependencies that are not explicitly managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for system-level orchestration, but the DP requires clear interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to avoid diffusion. Recommend explicit definition of: (1) phase timestamp format and timing guarantees from Vehicle, (2) latency metrics from Teleoperation, (3) safety constraint status and throttling response from Safety Services.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials with continuous telemetry capture. The refined FR specifies measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) and verification method (statistical analysis of cycle times under controlled duty cycle). NGHE Solution IM can aggregate telemetry and compute KPI metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the system-level KPI orchestration and phase budget allocation. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own subsystem-level execution and must meet their allocated budgets. Recommend explicit allocation document mapping each sub-FR to responsible module.

recommended_revisions: Define formal interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services, specifying: phase timestamp format, latency metrics, safety constraint status, and adaptive throttling commands.
Establish a cycle time KPI dashboard in NGHE Solution that aggregates telemetry and reports p95 metrics in real-time, with alerts if KPI is at risk.
Define hard safety limits for adaptive safety throttling (e.g., minimum safety margin that cannot be reduced), and require Safety Manager approval for any changes.
Create a fallback mode specification: if p95 ≤ CT_target cannot be achieved within safety constraints, define the escalation path (e.g., notify Site Manager, reduce duty cycle, or accept KPI miss).

decision.justification: The refined FR is decomposable into four sub-FRs (vehicle phase budgets, teleoperation latency, safety constraint overhead, and telemetry reporting), each of which requires further functional decomposition. The selected DP (phase budget decomposition) is sound and decoupled, but the IM assignment to NGHE Solution introduces orchestration complexity that must be validated through lower-level design. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify subsystem-level requirements and interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CT-001: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual phase time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-CT-002: The teleoperation system shall maintain operator command-to-vehicle-response latency ≤ latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-CT-003: Safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-CT-004: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI verification.

assumptions: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable.
Vehicle subsystems can be tuned to meet allocated phase budgets.
Teleoperation latency is measurable and controllable within network constraints.
Safety constraint overhead is quantifiable and can be adaptively throttled within hard safety limits.
Telemetry infrastructure has sufficient fidelity and bandwidth for real-time feedback.
Phase budget allocation is feasible without violating physical or operational constraints.
Adaptive safety throttling can be implemented without creating unsafe coupling between performance and safety.
open_risks: Risk: Vague or changing CT_target will invalidate budget allocation; mitigation: establish formal acceptance criteria and change control process.
Risk: Hardware limitations may make phase budgets infeasible; mitigation: conduct feasibility study during detailed design.
Risk: Network variability may exceed teleoperation latency budget; mitigation: characterize network performance and define fallback modes.
Risk: Safety margins may be non-negotiable, preventing adaptive reduction; mitigation: engage Safety Manager early to define throttling limits.
Risk: Telemetry infrastructure gaps may prevent real-time feedback; mitigation: conduct instrumentation gap analysis and define minimum telemetry requirements.
Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' with excessive orchestration logic; mitigation: clearly separate system-level orchestration from subsystem execution.
evidence_needed: Formal definition of CT_target, duty cycle, and site conditions from Site Manager.
Feasibility study confirming that vehicle phase budgets are achievable within hardware constraints.
Network performance characterization (latency, jitter, bandwidth) to validate teleoperation latency budget.
Safety analysis confirming that adaptive safety throttling can be implemented without violating safety criticality.
Telemetry instrumentation plan specifying phase timestamp sources, accuracy, and bandwidth requirements.
Interface control documents (ICDs) between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services.
Cycle time KPI dashboard prototype demonstrating real-time p95 metric computation and alerting.
